---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite borehole/well log strip section** (колонка скважин) — a long, vertically oriented geological log strip showing lithological columns from one or more boreholes, with correlation lines connecting equivalent stratigraphic horizons between wells. The format is characteristic of Soviet-era exploratory drilling documentation, presenting multiple parallel drill core logs side by side with connecting lines indicating stratigraphic correlations.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure appears to cover a **corridor or profile of multiple boreholes** drilled during the regional survey of northern Kazakhstan (map sheets M-42 / N-42). The strip extends over a considerable depth range — likely spanning several hundred meters to over 1,000 meters based on the length of the log columns. Individual borehole numbers (скважины) are labeled at the top of each column, though at this resolution the specific numbers (likely in the format скв-1, скв-2, etc.) are difficult to confirm with certainty. The columns are arranged along what appears to be a **northwest-to-southeast trending profile line**, consistent with regional seismic/drilling transects described in Report 36572.

---

## Key Content

The figure displays the following key elements:

- **Multiple vertical lithological columns** (минимум 6–8 скважин), each represented as a narrow rectangular strip filled with standardized lithological symbols (hatching patterns representing different rock types such as sandstone, claystone, limestone, crystalline basement, etc.)
- **Correlation lines** (линии корреляции) connecting equivalent stratigraphic boundaries across boreholes — these diagonal and near-horizontal lines are clearly visible running between columns, indicating the interpreter has identified **correlatable stratigraphic horizons** across the profile
- **Depth scale** running vertically along the left side and/or beside each column, with tick marks at regular intervals (likely every 50 or 100 meters)
- **Lithological pattern variations** within each column showing alternating sequences of:
  - Dense cross-hatching (likely crystalline or dense carbonate/igneous rocks)
  - Finer hatching or dotted patterns (likely sedimentary clastics — sandstones, siltstones)
  - Blank or lightly marked intervals (likely clays or unconsolidated sediments)
- **Red/orange highlighting** visible on portions of some columns in the upper section, possibly indicating **mineralized intervals**, zones of interest, or a specific formation marker
- **Branching correlation lines** in the lower half suggest the stratigraphy becomes more complex or uncertain at depth, with possible faulting or unconformities
- The overall geometry of the correlation lines suggests **gentle dipping or faulted stratigraphy**, with some horizons deepening progressively from one end of the profile to the other

---

## Text and Labels

At this image resolution, full transcription is limited, but the following elements are discernible:

- **Title block** at the top (upper right area): likely reads something similar to:
  *«Литологическая колонка скважин по профилю...»*
  (Lithological column of boreholes along profile...)
- **Borehole headers**: column headings above each strip with скважина numbers
- **Formation/horizon labels** along the right margin and between columns, identifying stratigraphic units (свиты, горизонты)
- **Depth annotations**: numeric depth values (in meters) marked at key boundaries along the columns
- **Legend block** (upper left): a small legend box explaining lithological symbols used in the columns
- **Blue stamp/label** (upper left area): appears to be an archival registration stamp, possibly reading:
  *«Фонд №...»* or similar archival notation
- **Red stamp** (mid-left): likely a security or classification marking standard for Soviet geological reports
- Annotations in Russian along correlation lines identifying formation names such as possibly:
  *«кора выветривания»* (weathering crust), *«кристаллический фундамент»* (crystalline basement), formation suite names (свита)

## Research Value

This figure is a primary subsurface data document that reveals the **lateral continuity and depth variation of stratigraphic units** across a transect in northern Kazakhstan, allowing a geologist to assess the geometry of sedimentary cover over crystalline basement — critical for evaluating uranium, coal, or hydrocarbon potential in platform settings. The correlation lines directly answer the question of **how key horizons (including potential ore-bearing or reservoir formations) correlate spatially between drill holes**, providing ground truth for interpreting seismic and gravity anomalies from the same survey. For an AI research assistant, this figure provides the core lithological and stratigraphic framework needed to understand subsurface architecture and prioritize zones of mineral interest within the M-42/N-42 survey area.
